maulgar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (894) ran well below the Top 100 average (2,200, a 41% ratio), on a strong 66th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 3rd of 4 by raw healing (Shlicktree's own Maulgar pull failed to capture any data this raid, narrowing the raw-healing comparison population by one - a real, single-kill data gap, not an error). 1 real death logged (Pointsetta).
maulgar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 89.9% of the Top 100 sample's healing) was never cast this kill - the single largest such gap of the entire raid - with Kilsby splitting healing between Flash of Light (57.3%) and a lower Holy Light rank (guid 25292, 42.7%) instead.
maulgar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock went uncast this kill (2% Top 100 usage, a genuinely rare cooldown on this short fight) - a real, complete non-use, not a guid-tracking artifact: its cast (guid 33072) and heal (guid 33074) are different real guids, but a zero cast count here means no ambiguity either way. No other cooldown was cast either, on a fight this short (37 seconds).
maulgar.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (80.8% vs 68.7% average, +12.1) ran above the Top 100 norm, across a narrow 4-target spread - a tighter-than-typical tunnel-heal pattern on this short burst-damage fight.

gruul.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (566) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,268, a 45% ratio), on a solid 65th-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 4th by raw healing. 1 real death logged (Vinnyvoss).
gruul.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 72.9% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead leaned heavily on Flash of Light (67.4%) with a lower Holy Light rank (28.4%) and Holy Shock (4.2%) filling in the rest.
gruul.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Divine Favor and Mana Potion both went uncast against real 73%/89% Top 100 usage norms - the largest cooldown gaps of the raid on this kill. Vinnyvoss's death has no cooldown nearby worth calling a save.
gruul.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (70.4% vs 78.1% average, -7.7) and top-target concentration (23.6% vs 43.2% average, -19.6) both ran below the Top 100 norm across 12 distinct targets.

hydross.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (504) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,438, a 35% ratio), on a middling 46th-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank but last (5th of 5) by raw healing despite that - the first of six kills this raid with that same gear-vs-output gap. 2 real deaths logged (Staubz, Deepbowlz).
hydross.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 69.8% Top 100 share); Kilsby split healing between Flash of Light (53.3%) and a lower Holy Light rank (44.7%) instead.
hydross.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here matched Top 100 norms - Holy Shock, Divine Favor, and Mana Potion all landed in line. Neither real death lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it - the nearest casts are incidental timing.
hydross.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (37.8% vs 87.9% average, -50.1) ran far below the Top 100 norm - the widest gap of the raid - across 11 distinct targets; the benchmark sample tunnels healing far more heavily onto a single target on this fight than Kilsby did here.

leotheras.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (813) ran below the Top 100 average (1,370, a 59% ratio), but this was a strong 86th-percentile kill, the raid's best by far - ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank but last (5th of 5) by raw healing, on the raid's most chaotic encounter (9 real deaths in a five-minute span, a kill that ultimately succeeded).
leotheras.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 71.1% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran Flash of Light heavily (77.2%) with a lower Holy Light rank (21.7%) and light Holy Shock use (1.1%).
leotheras.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Every tracked cooldown here (Holy Shock, Divine Favor, Divine Shield, Mana Potion, all self-cast once) matched Top 100 norms. With 9 real deaths in under five minutes, none lines up closely enough with a specific cooldown to call a real save - this reads as raid-wide burst damage, not a missed opportunity.
leotheras.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (47.4%) and top-target concentration (15.6%) both tracked close to the Top 100 norm, across the raid's widest spread (20 distinct targets) - consistent with the chaos of 9 real deaths.

karathress.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (622) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,622, a 38% ratio), on a weak 30th-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank but last (5th of 5) by raw healing. 2 real deaths logged (Staubz, Zuggnuggs).
karathress.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light carried 91.3% of this kill's healing, 81.6 points above its 9.7% Top 100 benchmark share - the largest single-spell gap of the raid - while the benchmark's own top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 77.0% there) went uncast entirely.
karathress.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock, Divine Favor, and Mana Potion were each cast once, all self-targeted, roughly in line with benchmark norms. Neither real death lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it.
karathress.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (74.9% vs 92.5% average, -17.6) and top-target concentration (45.5% vs 70.0% average, -24.5) both ran below the Top 100 norm across 17 distinct targets - the benchmark sample tunnels more heavily onto a single target on this fight than Kilsby did here.

morogrim.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (705) ran below the Top 100 average (1,226, a 58% ratio), on a solid 73rd-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank - the best item-level rank of the raid - though only 3rd by raw healing. 3 real deaths logged (Shlicktree, Deepbowlz, Vinnyvoss).
morogrim.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light carried 91.7% of this kill's healing, 75.3 points above its 16.4% Top 100 benchmark share; the benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 62.2% there) again went uncast entirely.
morogrim.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock, Divine Favor, and Divine Shield were each cast once (Divine Shield and Divine Favor self-targeted), roughly in line with benchmark norms. None of the 3 real deaths lines up closely with a cooldown that could plausibly have prevented it.
morogrim.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (53.3% vs 86.4% average, -33.1) and top-target concentration (20.1% vs 66.9% average, -46.8) both ran well below the Top 100 norm - the second-widest gap of the raid - across 23 distinct targets.

lurker.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (519) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,381, a 38% ratio), on a weak 37th-percentile kill, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and last (5th of 5) by raw healing, on a clean kill with zero real deaths.
lurker.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light carried 87.0% of this kill's healing, 72.2 points above its 14.8% Top 100 benchmark share; the benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 64.3% there) again went uncast entirely.
lurker.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Divine Favor and Mana Potion both went uncast against real 77%/80% Top 100 usage norms. Holy Shock was cast 4 times (3 self-targeted, the most self-focused Holy Shock use of the raid), well above its 35% Top 100 usage rate. No deaths to answer.
lurker.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (12.0% vs 60.9% average, -48.9) ran far below the Top 100 norm - the second-widest gap of the raid - across 20 distinct targets; Kilsby's own name appears among the top 5 healing targets (8.4%).

vashj.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (520) ran below the Top 100 average (948, a 55% ratio), on a middling 51st-percentile kill, ranking 2nd of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank and 4th by raw healing, with 6 real deaths on the raid's longest fight (543 seconds).
vashj.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: This is the one kill where Flash of Light's real Top 100 share (54.4%) already exceeds Holy Light's (30.0%), and Kilsby's own mix (83.4% Flash of Light) follows that same shape, just more heavily - the benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) still went uncast here, but by the smallest margin of the raid (-30.0 points).
vashj.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock (3 casts), Divine Favor, Divine Shield, Cleanse, and Mana Potion all landed roughly in line with Top 100 norms. None of the 6 real deaths lines up closely enough with a cooldown to call a real save.
vashj.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (39.8%) and top-target concentration (8.7%) both ran below the Top 100 norm across the raid's second-widest spread (23 distinct targets) - the least-concentrated single-target profile of the raid.

alar.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (452) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,018, a 44% ratio), on a decent 57th-percentile kill, ranking 3rd of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing, on a clean kill with zero real deaths. A minority-kill ring swap (item 29290 to 28763, both similar item level) shows up here and on Vashj only - a real, benign gear variant, not a downgrade worth flagging.
alar.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 50.5% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran Flash of Light heavily (73.2%) with a lower Holy Light rank (22.0%) and real Holy Shock use (4.8%).
alar.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Divine Favor and Mana Potion both went uncast against real 87%/96% Top 100 usage norms. Holy Shock was cast 3 times (2 self-targeted), above its 73% Top 100 usage rate. No deaths to answer.
alar.TARGET_FINDING: Top-target concentration (16.4% vs 37.4% average, -21.0) ran well below the Top 100 norm across 21 distinct targets.

voidreaver.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (1,085, the raid's highest raw HPS) still ran below the Top 100 average (1,780, a 61% ratio), on a strong 75th-percentile kill, ranking 1st of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank - yet last (5th of 5) by raw healing despite that, the sixth kill this raid with that same gear-vs-output gap. 2 real deaths logged (Captinspanky, Staubz).
voidreaver.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 66.3% Top 100 share); Kilsby split healing between Flash of Light (61.0%) and a lower Holy Light rank (39.0%) instead - no Holy Shock at all.
voidreaver.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock was never cast this kill (43% Top 100 usage) - a real, complete non-use, not a guid-tracking artifact: its cast (guid 33072) and heal (guid 33074) are different real guids, but a zero cast count here means no ambiguity either way. Both real deaths land within 15 seconds of the kill's only Divine Favor cast - a self-buff cooldown, not a rescue tool, so this reads as coincidental timing rather than a missed save.
voidreaver.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (53.1% vs 67.4% average, -14.3) and top-target concentration (16.2% vs 26.3% average, -10.1) both ran below the Top 100 norm across 20 distinct targets.

solarian.SCORECARD_FINDING: HPS (421) ran well below the Top 100 average (1,558, a 27% ratio - the weakest ratio of the raid), and overheal (59.5%) exceeds the sample's single worst parse - the only kill this raid where that happens - landing at a weak 32nd-percentile kill, ranking 4th of 5 tracked healers by both item-level rank and raw healing. 1 real death logged (Captinspanky).
solarian.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: The benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136) was never cast this kill (0% vs a real 69.2% Top 100 share); Kilsby instead ran Flash of Light heavily (77.0%) with real, above-benchmark Holy Shock use (10.0% vs a 1.3% Top 100 share, the heaviest Holy Shock reliance of the raid).
solarian.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock was cast twice, both aimed at Captinspanky (the kill's own single real death) - the first landed about 22 seconds before the death, the second about 21 seconds after; neither reads as a clean, successful save given the timing. Divine Favor and Mana Potion both went uncast.
solarian.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (51.6%) and top-target concentration (12.6%) both ran below the Top 100 norm across 20 distinct targets.

kaelthas.SCORECARD_FINDING: The raid's low point: HPS (341) ran to just 40% of the Top 100 average (846), producing a weak 17th-percentile result, the raid's lowest. Kilsby died about 4:17 into this 8-minute fight; Food was also inactive this kill (the only kill this raid without it), and active time fell to 74.3% (Top 100 average 83.5%). Kilsby ranked 4th of 5 tracked healers by item-level rank (despite the item level bracket rising to 131, up from 129 on every earlier kill) and last (5th of 5) by raw healing. 6 real deaths logged, including Kilsby's own.
kaelthas.SPELL_COMPOSITION_FINDING: Flash of Light carried 85.3% of this kill's healing, 49.5 points above its 35.8% Top 100 benchmark share - though this fight's own benchmark leans on Flash of Light more than any other boss this raid (35.8%, the highest Top 100 Flash of Light share); the benchmark's top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 44.8% there) again went uncast entirely.
kaelthas.COOLDOWN_FINDING: Holy Shock, Divine Shield, and Cleanse were each cast once; the Cleanse cast is unusual - it targeted Kael'thas Sunstrider itself, a real boss-targeted dispel event on this fight. Divine Favor went uncast (83% Top 100 usage). Kilsby's own death came 63 seconds before the kill's only Cleanse cast - too far removed to read as a failed self-save. Of the other 5 real deaths, only Churbert (3.3s from a Holy Shock cast) and Staubz (1.7s from a Mana Potion cast) land close to any cooldown, and both read as coincidental timing rather than real saves.
kaelthas.TARGET_FINDING: Coverage (39.3%) and top-target concentration (10.3%) both ran below the Top 100 norm - the least-concentrated single-target profile of the raid alongside Vashj - across the raid's widest spread (24 distinct targets).

RaidOverview.GEAR_CONSISTENCY_FINDING: Two slots vary, both real but benign single/minority-kill variants confirmed by the mechanical gear analysis, not flagged for review: the Finger2 ring (item 29290 to 28763, similar item level) on Vashj and Al'ar only, and the Tabard (a different guild tabard, item 23192 to 5976) on Kael'thas only.
RaidOverview.GEAR_FINDING_NOTE: 8 of 9 enchantable slots carry a permanent enchant on every kill; the Back slot (Cape, item 28765) never does - the one persistent gear gap, matching Kilsby's other report. Guardian Elixir was never active on any of the 12 kills this raid night either, and neither was Flask - only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) and Well Fed food were the norm, and even food dropped out on Kael'thas specifically, the one kill with no food buff at all.
RaidOverview.RAID_SUMMARY_FINDING: All 12 real bosses attempted this raid night were killed. Percentiles ranged from 17th (Kael'thas, the raid's lowest) to 86th (Leotheras, the raid's best), with Kael'thas, Karathress (30th), and Solarian (32nd) the three lowest kills. Kilsby ranked 1st of 5 tracked healers present by item-level rank on 2 of 12 kills (Morogrim, Void Reaver; 53% average rank percentile) but never once ranked 1st by raw healing across all 12 - landing dead last (5th of 5) on 6 of them (Hydross, Leotheras, Karathress, Lurker, Void Reaver, Kael'thas), an even more pronounced version of the same gear-vs-raw-output gap seen in Kilsby's other report on this raid tier. The Top 100 benchmark's dominant top-rank Holy Light (guid 27136, 840 mana) was never cast once across all 12 kills again this raid night - but this time Kilsby leaned almost entirely on Flash of Light rather than balancing it with a lower Holy Light rank, reaching as high as 91.7% of a single kill's healing (Morogrim) - a real, more extreme version of the same underlying pattern. Only 1 kill (Solarian) saw overheal exceed the Top 100 sample's own worst parse, a real improvement over Kilsby's other raid night. Kilsby died once this raid, on Kael'thas (about 4:17 into that fight) - the raid's low point by percentile too. Leotheras (9 real deaths) and a Kael'thas/Vashj tie (6 deaths each) were the raid's most chaotic kills.
RaidOverview.RAID_WARNING_BANNER: Guardian Elixir was never active on any of the 12 kills this raid night, and Food dropped out too on Kael'thas specifically - a real, incomplete consumable setup, matching the same gap in Kilsby's other report. Despite ranking 1st by item-level among the 5 tracked healers on 2 kills, Kilsby never ranked 1st by raw healing on any of the 12 - landing last on half of them.

GearCheckItems.Description: No Guardian Elixir active on any kill; no Flask either; Food also missing on Kael'thas
GearCheckItems.LongDetail: Only Battle Elixir (Healing Power) was consistently active across all 12 kills this raid night, alongside Well Fed food on 11 of 12 (missing on Kael'thas). Guardian Elixir was never up. This is the same incomplete consumable pattern already confirmed in Kilsby's other report for this raid tier.
